title: dryopteris patula var. chaerophylloides
intro: Dryopteris patula var. chaerophylloides, also known as the hybrid golden shield fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris patula fern plant, characterized by its unique fronds with golden or copper-colored scales. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dryopteris patula var. chaerophylloides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Dryopteris patula var. chaerophylloides is typically prepared for herbal use by drying the fronds and infusing them in hot water to make a tea, or by using the plant in tincture or capsule form.
